perturbation.original_text: 3. The Receiving Party expressly agrees not to use the Confidential Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider whether to enter into a Transaction and shall strictly limit its disclosure to such of its employees, directors and advisors having a need to know such information, which parties shall be advised that such information is Confidential Information and subject to the terms of this Agreement. Except as set forth herein, the Receiving Party shall hold all information received in confidence and not sell, assign, transfer, release or otherwise disclose the Confidential Information, or material derived therefrom, to any third party, or to its other employees, officers, directors, shareholders, agents or consultants. Notwithstanding the termination of this Agreement for any reason, the Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for purposes of competing with the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The Receiving Party expressly agrees not to use the Confidential Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider whether to enter into a Transaction. Except as set forth herein, the Receiving Party shall hold all information received in confidence and not sell, assign, transfer, release or otherwise disclose the Confidential Information, or material derived therefrom, to any third party, or to its other employees, officers, directors, shareholders, agents or consultants. Notwithstanding the termination of this Agreement for any reason, the Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for purposes of competing with the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "and shall strictly limit its disclosure to such of its employees, directors and advisors having a need to know such information, which parties shall be advised that such information is Confidential Information and subject to the terms of this Agreement" from Section 3, the amended clause now becomes ambiguous about who can have access to confidential information, especially considering the text says 'the Receiving Party shall hold all information received in confidence and not sell, assign, transfer, release or otherwise disclose the Confidential Information'. This is in contradiction with Section 5, which contemplates that the Receiving Party might transmit the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to others.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. In the event that the Receiving Party or anyone to whom the Receiving Party transmits the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement becomes legally compelled to disclose all or any portion of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information or to disclose the existence of Negotiations, the Receiving Party will provide the Disclosing Party with prompt notice thereof, so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, the Receiving Party or the recipient of such Disclosing Party's Confidential Information will furnish only that portion of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information or aspect of the Negotiations which is legally required to be disclosed and the Receiving Party, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, will exercise its reasonable efforts to obtain reliable assurances that confidential treatment will be afforded such portion of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 3 explicitly limits disclosure of Confidential Information to specific individuals with a need to know, ensuring they are aware of its confidential nature. The changed text removes this limitation, creating ambiguity about who can access the information, while still stating that the Receiving Party shall hold all information in confidence. This contradicts Section 5, which anticipates that the Receiving Party might transmit the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to others, implying a controlled disclosure process. By removing the disclosure restriction in Section 3, it creates a contradiction because Section 5 relies on a framework of permissible transmission that is now undefined in Section 3. The removal introduces ambiguity where Section 5 expects a specific process.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Except as must be disclosed pursuant to applicable legal disclosure requirements or legal process, neither party nor any of its respective representatives may, without the prior written consent of the other party, disclose to any person (other than to its employees, directors and advisors having a need to know such information) that the parties have exchanged confidential information, are engaged in negotiations for a Transaction, or the proposed terms of a Transaction (collectively, the "Negotiations") until such information has been disclosed by an appropriate legal filing and/or press release mutually agreed upon by SER and EIS. Each party shall undertake all necessary and reasonable steps to ensure that the Negotiations remain secret and confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Neither party nor any of its respective representatives may, without the prior written consent of the other party, disclose to any person (other than to its employees, directors and advisors) that the parties have exchanged confidential information, are engaged in negotiations for a Transaction, or the proposed terms of a Transaction (collectively, the "Negotiations") until such information has been disclosed by an appropriate legal filing and/or press release mutually agreed upon by SER and EIS. Each party shall undertake all necessary steps to ensure that the Negotiations remain secret and confidential.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of "Except as must be disclosed pursuant to applicable legal disclosure requirements or legal process" creates a contradiction with section 5. It now unconditionally prohibits disclosure, conflicting with section 5 which outlines procedures when disclosure is legally compelled. In Section 5, it is accepted that there is a case where the Negotiations can be disclosed. Now, with the change in section 4, the text in section 4 and 5 become contradictory
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. In the event that the Receiving Party or anyone to whom the Receiving Party transmits the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement becomes legally compelled to disclose all or any portion of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information or to disclose the existence of Negotiations, the Receiving Party will provide the Disclosing Party with prompt notice thereof, so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, the Receiving Party or the recipient of such Disclosing Party's Confidential Information will furnish only that portion of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information or aspect of the Negotiations which is legally required to be disclosed and the Receiving Party, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, will exercise its reasonable efforts to obtain reliable assurances that confidential treatment will be afforded such portion of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 4 allowed for disclosure pursuant to legal disclosure requirements or legal process. The changed text removes this exception, creating an unconditional prohibition on disclosure. This directly contradicts section 5, which outlines the procedure to be followed when a party is legally compelled to disclose information. Therefore, the removal of the exception in section 4 directly creates a contradiction with section 5, as one section prohibits disclosure under all circumstances while the other section anticipates and addresses the circumstances of legally compelled disclosure. This satisfies the condition for a YES response because it introduces a clear and direct conflict that affects the obligations and enforcability of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 7. The Receiving Party agrees that all Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and no license or other rights in the Confidential Information is granted hereby. The Receiving Party will not disclose the existence, content and/or substance of any of the Confidential Information to any third party; nor develop, manufacture, produce and/or distribute any software product(s) derived from or which otherwise use any of the Confidential Information. The Disclosing Party agrees to return (and have any third party to who it supplied the Confidential Information return) to the Receiving Party, immediately upon notification by the Disclosing Party in writing, all Confidential Information, including but not limited to all computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies or reproductions thereof, in any form or medium whatsoever, and not to retain any copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. The Receiving Party agrees that all Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and no license or other rights in the Confidential Information is granted hereby. The Receiving Party will not disclose the existence, content and/or substance of any of the Confidential Information to any third party; nor develop, manufacture, produce and/or distribute any software product(s) derived from or which otherwise use any of the Confidential Information. The Disclosing Party agrees to return (and have any third party to who it supplied the Confidential Information return) to the Receiving Party, at the conclusion of the transaction, all Confidential Information, including but not limited to all computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies or reproductions thereof, in any form or medium whatsoever, and not to retain any copies thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the return of Confidential Information to happen "at the conclusion of the transaction", there now exists a conflict with the end of section 1. Where it states 'Notwithstanding the termination of this Agreement for any reason, the Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for purposes of competing with the Disclosing Party.', since the agreement can be terminated for any reason, including before the transaction has concluded, there are now 2 different times to return the Confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. The Receiving Party expressly agrees not to use the Confidential Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider whether to enter into a Transaction and shall strictly limit its disclosure to such of its employees, directors and advisors having a need to know such information, which parties shall be advised that such information is Confidential Information and subject to the terms of this Agreement. Except as set forth herein, the Receiving Party shall hold all information received in confidence and not sell, assign, transfer, release or otherwise disclose the Confidential Information, or material derived therefrom, to any third party, or to its other employees, officers, directors, shareholders, agents or consultants. Notwithstanding the termination of this Agreement for any reason, the Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for purposes of competing with the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the timing of returning Confidential Information to "at the conclusion of the transaction," whereas the original text required immediate return upon written notification. This directly contradicts the clause in the contradicted text stating that 'Notwithstanding the termination of this Agreement for any reason, the Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for purposes of competing with the Disclosing Party', since the agreement can be terminated for any reason, including before the transaction has concluded. This change introduces a conflict because the agreement may terminate before the transaction concludes, leaving ambiguity about when the information should be returned, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as it now implies different timelines and conditions for returning the information based on termination or transaction completion.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Although the Disclosing Party shall endeavor to provide the other party with information relevant for an evaluation of a Transaction, the Disclosing Party and its advisors have not made, and will not make, any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the information being provided. Any and all such representations and warranties shall be only as specifically set forth in a definitive agreement, if one is hereafter executed and delivered in connection with a Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. The Disclosing Party shall endeavor to provide the other party with information relevant for an evaluation of a Transaction, the Disclosing Party and its advisors have not made, and will not make, any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the information being provided. Any and all such representations and warranties shall be only as specifically set forth in a definitive agreement, if one is hereafter executed and delivered in connection with a Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'Although' in the beginning of the original section, the altered section 11 makes the provision of information relevant for the evaluation of a Transaction now a condition and duty that the Disclosing party 'shall' ensure. This is in contradiction with section 1, where it states 'each party may furnish to the other certain information'. Changing the duty of providing the information in section 11, makes section 1 and 11 contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Each party agrees that all information disclosed by a party (the "Disclosing Party"), including without limitation information acquired by the other party (the "Receiving Party") from Disclosing Party's employees or upon inspection of Disclosing Party's property, relating (without limitation) to Disclosing Party's products, designs, business opportunities, plans (business, marketing or otherwise), strategies, budgets, finance, customer lists, contracts, research and development, software programs, trade secrets, know-how, techniques, inventions, processes, distribution methods, schematics and personnel disclosed to Disclosing Party by third parties, shall be considered "Confidential Information".
perturbation.justification: The original text stated, "Although the Disclosing Party shall endeavor...", implying a conditional obligation. The changed text removes "Although", making the endeavor a direct obligation. This creates a contradiction with section 1, which states that each party 'may furnish' information, implying an option, not a requirement. The change in section 11 transforms a conditional attempt to provide information into a mandatory action, contradicting the permissive nature of information sharing in section 1. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' because the obligation to disclose information is defined inconsistently, making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Without the written consent of the other party, neither party nor its affiliates shall not solicit for employment, nor shall it employ or retain or enter into any contract for the provision of personal services during the two (2) year period commencing on the date hereof any person who is employed by the other party as of the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Without the written consent of the other party, neither party nor its affiliates shall not solicit for employment, nor shall it employ or retain or enter into any contract for the provision of personal services during the two (2) year period commencing on the date hereof any person who is employed by the other party as of the date hereof, except if they are consultants.
perturbation.explanation: Adding "except if they are consultants" to section 12 introduces a contradiction with the original intention of the text. The original text says 'neither party nor its affiliates shall not solicit for employment, nor shall it employ or retain or enter into any contract for the provision of personal services during the two (2) year period', which clearly prohibits retaining anyone from the other party. Adding "except if they are consultants" is now introducing a way for parties to retain anyone under personal services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Without the written consent of the other party, neither party nor its affiliates shall not solicit for employment, nor shall it employ or retain or enter into any contract for the provision of personal services during the two (2) year period commencing on the date hereof any person who is employed by the other party as of the date hereof, except if they are consultants.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 12 prohibits the solicitation, employment, retaining, or contracting for personal services of anyone employed by the other party for two years. The changed text adds 'except if they are consultants.' This directly contradicts the original intent by introducing an exception that allows the prohibited actions under certain circumstances. The contradiction exists because the original text creates an explicit restriction, while the changed text creates an exception to that restriction.
